---
title: Model your data
description: Choose a CRDT collection for a field of your app state and read and write it.
sidebar:
  order: 2
---

import { Aside, CardGrid, LinkCard } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

**Task:** store a piece of your app's data in a field of `#[app::state]`, and
read and write it from a method.

Every field of your state is a CRDT from `calimero_storage::collections`. Pick
the one whose merge behaviour matches the data — that single choice decides how
concurrent edits on different nodes converge.

## Pick a collection

| You have… | Use | Import |
| --- | --- | --- |
| A keyed map of values | `UnorderedMap<K, V>` | `calimero_storage::collections::UnorderedMap` |
| A growable list | `Vector<V>` | `calimero_storage::collections::Vector` |
| A map you iterate in key order / by range | `SortedMap<K, V>` | `calimero_storage::collections::SortedMap` |
| A count many nodes bump | `Counter` (grow-only) / `PNCounter` (also decrements) | `calimero_storage::collections::Counter` |
| A single value, last-writer-wins | `LwwRegister<T>` | `calimero_storage::collections::LwwRegister` |

<Aside type="tip" title="Wrap plain values in LwwRegister">
  A bare `String` or `u64` isn't itself a CRDT. To store a single overwritable
  value — including as the *value type* of a map — wrap it in `LwwRegister<T>`
  so concurrent overwrites resolve deterministically.
</Aside>

See [Collections](/build/collections/) for the full list and the exact merge
rule of each.

## Read and write it

This is `apps/kv-store` — an `UnorderedMap` whose values are `LwwRegister<String>`:

```rust
use calimero_sdk::app;
use calimero_storage::collections::{LwwRegister, UnorderedMap};

#[app::state]
pub struct KvStore {
    items: UnorderedMap<String, LwwRegister<String>>,
}

#[app::logic]
impl KvStore {
    #[app::init]
    pub fn init() -> KvStore {
        KvStore { items: UnorderedMap::new() }
    }

    // write: `value.into()` builds the LwwRegister<String>
    pub fn set(&mut self, key: String, value: String) -> app::Result<()> {
        self.items.insert(key, value.into())?;
        Ok(())
    }

    // read: `get` returns a read-only ValueRef; clone out the inner value
    #[app::view]
    pub fn get(&self, key: String) -> app::Result<Option<String>> {
        Ok(self.items.get(&key)?.map(|v| v.get().clone()))
    }
}
```

Key things the snippet shows:

- Construct fields with `::new()` inside `#[app::init]`.
- Every accessor returns a `Result` — collection reads and writes can fail, so
  use `?`.
- `get` hands back a read-only reference; `.get().clone()` pulls the value out of
  the `LwwRegister`. To mutate in place, use `get_mut`.

## Exercise it

```sh
meroctl --node node1 call set \
  --context <context-id> --args '{"key": "hello", "value": "world"}'

meroctl --node node1 call get \
  --context <context-id> --args '{"key": "hello"}'
```
